steps.title: Find the enlargement factor
steps.content:
The new side length is 90% longer than the original: $$55 \text{ inches} \times (1 + 0.90) = 55 \times 1.9 = 104.5 \text{ inches}$$
The map has been enlarged by a linear factor of 1.9.

steps.title: Recognize the inverse scale relationship
steps.content:
The enlarged map represents the same geographic region as the original, just printed larger.

The original map's side length (55 inches) corresponds to $$55 \times 13 = 715 \text{ miles}$$
The enlarged map shows this same 715-mile region in 104.5 inches, so the new scale is $$\frac{715 \text{ miles}}{104.5 \text{ inches}} = \frac{13}{1.9} \text{ miles per inch}$$

steps.title: Calculate the new scale
steps.content: $$\frac{13}{1.9} = 6.842...$$, which rounds to 6.84 miles per inch. That is the correct answer.
